change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API

en.value: Activities:1.1) Conduct orientations for beneficiaries regarding the project and the concept of the “Market oriented Production” 1.2) Form/ reform Agriculture Groups and select “Experienced Leader Farmers (ELF)” from 68 different groups (expected), who are expected to play key roles linking the project and other farmers  1.3) Recruit and train local on-the-job trainees from Pre-diploma in Agriculture Plant Science (PJTA) and Junior Technical Assistant (JTA), who will be mobilized for technical follow-up among farmers 1.4) Organize study tours targeting the selected ELF for promoting sensitization on “Market oriented Production” 1.5) Conduct a series of training to ELF for operating and managing the Agriculture Groups 1.6) Support the ELF to conduct “Mini Seminar” in their monthly group meeting to disseminate gained knowledge and skills to other farmers  1.7) Facilitate Agriculture Groups and Cooperatives for legal registration at the Rural Municipality (RM) 1.8) Organize a "Joint Meeting" involving stakeholders in the agriculture sector to establish a supportive mechanism for revitalizing the agriculture at the local level 1.9) Organize an event to promote agriculture (International coffee day and Farmers Day) 1.10) Farmer Leadership Exchange Program   2.1) Select the direct beneficiaries (see “9. Direct beneficiary” below) 2.2) Organize “Study Tours” targeting the selected direct beneficiaries (Coffee/ Vegetables)  2.3) Conduct orientations in each Agriculture Group for crop selection, seasonal calendar and action plan in reflecting the tendency of market  2.4) Conduct soil pH test and provide technical support for soil management 2.5) Conduct training for skill upgrade (Coffee /Vegetables)- cultivation, compost preparation, pest management, orchard management, harvesting, record keeping for farm management 2.6) Provide the selected farmers with agricultural materials (sapling, seed, water drum, plastic sheet for nursery, bio-fertilizer, bio-pesticide, irrigation support, etc.) 2.7) Conduct training for coffee nursery management and provide material/ technical supports for establishing coffee nurseries  2.8) Develop and distribute a farmers’ record book for keeping expenses and incomes      3.1) Conduct training for existing coffee farmers regarding “Processing”  3.2) Provide technical and material supports for establishing a pulping center around 26 places in the target area   3.3) Facilitate Agriculture Group to develop a more effective production and sales mechanism  3.4) Identify new/existing buyers related to market   3.5) Support ELF to establish proactive relationship between buyers   3.6) Support for effective working modality and management group Coordination and management- Hold coordination meetings with central and local governments - Conduct SWC midterm and final evaluation - Hold project team meetings - Conduct social audit - Conduct BLS and ELS

en.full: Project for Promoting Market-oriented Agriculture through Coffee and Vegetable Farming for Sustainable Communities

version_summary.change_description: Initial sourcing from MoF DFMIS API
